title: fix: block native api key redirect metadata
author: its3li
pr_body_excerpt: Summary: Blocks native Better Auth api-key create/update routes from accepting redirect metadata directly. This keeps BYOP redirect allowlists on the Pollinations API-key routes where scheme and loopback validation runs. Testing: full api-keys integration file passed, targeted native redirect metadata test passed, git diff check passed. Note: local typecheck still fails on existing frontend UI package resolution and React DTS issues before this change.

gist.headline: Tightening the keyhole
gist.blurb: We've tightened up how API keys handle redirect metadata to keep everything safe and sound. Just a little backend housekeeping to keep the hive secure!
gist.summary: Blocked native Better Auth API-key routes from accepting redirect metadata directly to ensure all requests pass through Pollinations' scheme and loopback validation.
gist.impact: This prevents potential bypasses of redirect allowlists during API key creation and updates, improving overall platform security.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a friendly security guard or gatekeeper, checking a clipboard next to a glowing key or lock, representing the tightened validation on API key routes.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny security guard hat, holding a clipboard next to a glowing lime green (#ecf874) keyhole. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
